name: AZQ Stage 1 Wave B Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 1 Wave B so the Codex runner can evaluate migration and command-layer refactors without hardcoding all task logic.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the patch.

baseline_checks.name: storage_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the Finis storage module still imports cleanly.

STAGE1-WAVEB-01.description: Add canonical goal write helpers to storage.

checks.name: goal_write_helper_exists
checks.description: Verify storage.py exposes a canonical single-record write helper.
checks.code:
from azq.finis import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['write_goal', 'save_goal', 'upsert_goal']), 'missing canonical goal write helper'

checks.name: goal_write_helper_writes_canonical_file
checks.description: Verify one normalized goal can be persisted as a canonical FINIS markdown file.
checks.code:
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.finis import storage
writer = getattr(storage, 'write_goal', None) or getattr(storage, 'save_goal', None) or getattr(storage, 'upsert_goal', None)
assert writer is not None, 'write helper not found'
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    storage.GOALS_DIR = tmp_path / 'goals'
    storage.LEGACY_GOALS_FILE = tmp_path / 'goals.json'
    record = {
        'goal_id': 'FINIS_123',
        'title': 'Write helper test',
        'status': 'active',
        'created': '2026-01-01',
        'description': 'desc',
        'derived_from': ['spark_001']
    }
    goal_path = writer(record)
    assert goal_path == storage.goal_file_path('FINIS_123')
    assert goal_path.is_file(), 'canonical goal file was not written'
    text = goal_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    assert '# FINIS_123' in text
    assert '- title: Write helper test' in text
    assert '- derived_from:' in text

human_checks: The write helper rewrites exactly one canonical file per goal record.
Write-side storage ownership stays in azq/finis/storage.py rather than leaking back into command modules.

STAGE1-WAVEB-02.description: Implement one-way legacy migration into canonical goal files.

checks.name: legacy_migration_exists
checks.description: Verify storage.py exposes a dedicated one-way legacy migration helper.
checks.code:
from azq.finis import storage
assert any(hasattr(storage, name) for name in ['migrate_legacy_goals', 'migrate_legacy_goals_json', 'migrate_legacy_to_canonical_goals']), 'missing legacy migration helper'

checks.name: legacy_migration_preserves_ids_and_is_idempotent
checks.description: Verify legacy JSON records migrate into canonical files without changing ids or duplicating files on repeat runs.
checks.code:
import json
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.finis import storage
migrate = getattr(storage, 'migrate_legacy_goals', None) or getattr(storage, 'migrate_legacy_goals_json', None) or getattr(storage, 'migrate_legacy_to_canonical_goals', None)
assert migrate is not None, 'migration helper not found'
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    storage.GOALS_DIR = tmp_path / 'goals'
    storage.LEGACY_GOALS_FILE = tmp_path / 'goals.json'
    storage.LEGACY_GOALS_FILE.write_text(json.dumps([
        {'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'goal': 'First legacy goal', 'status': 'active'},
        {'goal_id': 'FINIS_002', 'goal': 'Second legacy goal', 'status': 'archived', 'derived_from': ['spark_2']}
    ]), encoding='utf-8')
    first = migrate()
    second = migrate()
    files = sorted(path.name for path in storage.GOALS_DIR.glob('FINIS_*.md'))
    assert files == ['FINIS_001.md', 'FINIS_002.md']
    assert first['migrated'] == 2, first
    assert second['migrated'] == 0, second
    assert second['skipped'] == 2, second
    loaded = {item['goal_id']: item for item in storage.load_all_goals()}
    assert set(loaded) == {'FINIS_001', 'FINIS_002'}
    assert loaded['FINIS_001']['title'] == 'First legacy goal'
    assert loaded['FINIS_002']['status'] == 'archived'

human_checks: Migration remains one-way and additive rather than trying to sync canonical files back into goals.json.
Idempotence comes from exact canonical file detection, not from fuzzy data merging.

STAGE1-WAVEB-03.description: Choose and wire the migration trigger.

checks.name: canonical_migration_trigger_exists
checks.description: Verify storage.py exposes one inspectable migration trigger with an explicit triggered flag.
checks.code:
import json
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory
from azq.finis import storage
trigger = getattr(storage, 'ensure_canonical_goals_migrated', None) or getattr(storage, 'ensure_goals_migrated', None)
assert trigger is not None, 'missing migration trigger helper'
with TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    tmp_path = Path(tmp)
    storage.GOALS_DIR = tmp_path / 'goals'
    storage.LEGACY_GOALS_FILE = tmp_path / 'goals.json'
    storage.LEGACY_GOALS_FILE.write_text(json.dumps([
        {'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'goal': 'Trigger test', 'status': 'active'}
    ]), encoding='utf-8')
    first = trigger()
    second = trigger()
    assert first['triggered'] is True, first
    assert first['migrated'] == 1, first
    assert second['triggered'] is False, second
    assert second['skipped'] == 1, second

checks.name: cli_wires_storage_ready_for_active_finis_commands
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/cli.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert 'def ensure_finis_storage_ready' in text
for snippet in [
    "elif cmd == \"fine\":\n        ensure_finis_storage_ready()",
    "elif cmd == \"goals\":\n        ensure_finis_storage_ready()",
    "elif cmd == \"goal\":\n",
    "        ensure_finis_storage_ready()"
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
PY
checks.description: Verify the CLI runs the storage migration trigger before active Finis command flows.

human_checks: The migration trigger is easy to find and reason about during debugging.
The chosen trigger strategy does not broaden into unrelated command refactors.

STAGE1-WAVEB-04.description: Refactor azq goals into a thin presentation layer.

checks.name: goals_module_is_thin_presentation_layer
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/finis/goals.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert 'load_all_goals' in text
assert 'def load_goals' not in text
assert 'def save_goals' not in text
assert 'def next_goal_id' not in text
PY
checks.description: Verify goals.py no longer owns duplicated JSON-era storage helpers.

checks.name: show_goals_reads_canonical_titles_and_filters_active
checks.description: Verify show_goals() reads canonical records, prints title, and preserves no-goals versus no-active behavior.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.finis.goals import show_goals
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.finis.goals.load_all_goals', return_value=[]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        show_goals()
assert 'No goals defined.' in buf.getvalue()
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.finis.goals.load_all_goals', return_value=[{'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Dormant', 'status': 'archived'}]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        show_goals()
assert 'No active goals.' in buf.getvalue()
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.finis.goals.load_all_goals', return_value=[
    {'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Active title', 'status': 'active'},
    {'goal_id': 'FINIS_002', 'title': 'Archived title', 'status': 'archived'}
]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        show_goals()
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'Active title' in out
assert 'Archived title' not in out
assert 'FINIS_001' in out

human_checks: goals.py stays focused on display logic rather than becoming a second storage layer.
The visible output is still simple and readable for terminal use.

STAGE1-WAVEB-05.description: Refactor goal_manager into a thin command-layer writer.

checks.name: goal_manager_no_longer_owns_json_storage
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/finis/goal_manager.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert 'load_goal' in text and 'write_goal' in text and 'next_goal_id' in text
assert 'goals.json' not in text
assert 'def load_goals' not in text
assert 'def save_goals' not in text
PY
checks.description: Verify goal_manager.py delegates persistence to storage.py rather than keeping JSON-era helpers.

checks.name: goal_manager_commands_use_exact_ids_and_canonical_records
checks.description: Verify add_goal(), close_goal(), and archive_goal() coordinate canonical storage operations with exact id lookup.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.finis.goal_manager import add_goal, close_goal, archive_goal
written = []
with patch('builtins.input', side_effect=['New canonical goal', 'optional description']), patch('azq.finis.goal_manager.next_goal_id', return_value='FINIS_010'), patch('azq.finis.goal_manager.write_goal', side_effect=lambda record: written.append(dict(record))):
    buf = io.StringIO()
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        add_goal()
assert written and written[0]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_010'
assert written[0]['title'] == 'New canonical goal'
assert written[0]['description'] == 'optional description'
assert written[0]['derived_from'] == []
assert written[0]['status'] == 'active'
with patch('azq.finis.goal_manager.load_goal', return_value={'goal_id': 'FINIS_010', 'title': 'Existing', 'status': 'active', 'created': '2026-01-01', 'description': '', 'derived_from': []}), patch('azq.finis.goal_manager.write_goal', side_effect=lambda record: written.append(dict(record))):
    close_goal('FINIS_010')
assert written[-1]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_010'
assert written[-1]['status'] == 'completed'
with patch('azq.finis.goal_manager.load_goal', return_value={'goal_id': 'FINIS_011', 'title': 'Existing', 'status': 'active', 'created': '2026-01-01', 'description': '', 'derived_from': []}), patch('azq.finis.goal_manager.write_goal', side_effect=lambda record: written.append(dict(record))):
    archive_goal('FINIS_011')
assert written[-1]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_011'
assert written[-1]['status'] == 'archived'

human_checks: Status transitions only rewrite the addressed goal file, not the whole repository state.
Manual goal creation stays minimal and readable at the command layer.

STAGE1-WAVEB-06.description: Refactor fine onto canonical storage and title-based dedupe.

checks.name: fine_imports_canonical_storage_instead_of_legacy_goals
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/finis/fine.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert 'from .goals import' not in text
assert 'load_all_goals' in text or 'load_goal' in text
assert 'write_goal' in text
PY
checks.description: Verify fine.py no longer depends on JSON-era helper imports from goals.py.

checks.name: fine_uses_canonical_titles_and_file_backed_writes
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/finis/fine.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
assert "g['goal']" not in text
assert '"goal":' not in text
assert "g['title']" in text or '"title":' in text
assert 'goals.json' not in text
PY
checks.description: Verify fine.py uses canonical title fields and no longer writes accepted goals to goals.json.

checks.name: fine_run_uses_canonical_records
checks.description: Verify run_fine() can dedupe against canonical titles and write confirmed goals one file at a time.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.finis.fine import run_fine, propose_goals
existing = [
    {'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Improve testing', 'status': 'active', 'created': '2026-01-01', 'description': '', 'derived_from': ['spark_old']}
]
candidates = propose_goals([
    {'spark': 'I want to improve testing', 'source': 'spark_dup'},
    {'spark': 'I want to improve deployment', 'source': 'spark_new'}
])
assert len(candidates) == 1, candidates
assert candidates[0]['title'] == 'Improve deployment'
written = []
with patch('azq.finis.fine.load_sparks', return_value=[{'spark': 'I want to improve deployment', 'source': 'spark_new'}]), patch('azq.finis.fine.load_all_goals', return_value=existing), patch('azq.finis.fine.confirm_goals', return_value=[{'title': 'Improve deployment', 'source': 'spark_new'}]), patch('azq.finis.fine.next_goal_id', return_value='FINIS_002'), patch('azq.finis.fine.write_goal', side_effect=lambda record: written.append(dict(record))):
    buf = io.StringIO()
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        run_fine()
assert written and written[0]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_002'
assert written[0]['title'] == 'Improve deployment'
assert written[0]['derived_from'] == ['spark_new']
assert written[0]['status'] == 'active'

human_checks: Candidate dedupe still behaves conservatively enough to avoid obvious near-duplicate goals.
fine.py now looks like a command-layer workflow over storage.py rather than a second persistence path.

checks.name: legacy_modules_no_longer_define_json_storage_helpers
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
for rel in ['azq/finis/goals.py', 'azq/finis/goal_manager.py', 'azq/finis/fine.py']:
    text = Path(rel).read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    assert 'def load_goals' not in text, rel
    assert 'def save_goals' not in text, rel
    assert 'def next_goal_id' not in text, rel
PY
checks.description: Verify legacy modules no longer define duplicated JSON-era persistence helpers.

checks.name: legacy_modules_no_longer_reference_legacy_goal_storage
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
for rel in ['azq/finis/goals.py', 'azq/finis/goal_manager.py', 'azq/finis/fine.py']:
    text = Path(rel).read_text(encoding='utf-8')
    assert 'goals.json' not in text, rel
    assert 'from .goals import' not in text, rel
PY
checks.description: Verify the active Finis command modules no longer route persistence through legacy goal storage paths.

human_checks: There is one obvious shared storage authority for Finis persistence after this cleanup.
Any remaining helper in the command modules is presentation or workflow logic, not hidden persistence.
